meta.title: Felipe Bell Marques — Full Stack Developer
meta.description: Full Stack Developer specializing in PHP, Laravel, and WordPress — enterprise platforms, custom frameworks, multi-database integrations, and CI/CD pipelines.

hero.name: Felipe Bell Marques
hero.tagline: I build enterprise web platforms with PHP, Laravel, and WordPress — from legacy migrations to custom frameworks with full CI/CD pipelines.

about.heading: About
paragraphs: I'm a Full Stack Developer based in Mogi Mirim, São Paulo, Brazil, with over a decade of experience spanning mainframe systems to modern web platforms. My core expertise is PHP — from WordPress and Drupal to Laravel and object-oriented architecture — combined with JavaScript, TypeScript, jQuery, and Angular on the frontend, plus Docker, CI/CD, and multi-database integration (MySQL, Oracle, PostgreSQL).

My career started in an unusual place: developing mainframe systems with COBOL, JCL, CICS, and DB2 at IBM for clients like AT&T and Bank Safra. That foundation taught me how large-scale, mission-critical systems work. I later spent five years at Stefanini building portals, plugins, and custom solutions for international clients like American Airlines, GSK, Edenred, and Sesc.

To truly master object-oriented PHP, I built my own MVC framework from scratch — and today it powers a full back-office operations platform I designed and built for a Brazilian supermarket chain with 8+ stores, complete with a Docker development environment, GitHub Actions CI/CD, RBAC, and real-time integrations with Oracle RMS and Senior ERP. I'm fluent in English, intermediate in Spanish, and passionate about sharing knowledge: I've given company-wide lectures on Docker and WordPress, and taught weekly English classes to colleagues.

roles.title: Systems Analyst & Full-Stack Developer
roles.description: Designed and built the company's back-office operations platform end to end — architecture, infrastructure, CI/CD, and features — centralizing retail workflows across 8+ supermarket stores. Built on my own hand-rolled PHP MVC framework with a multi-database layer connecting MySQL and four Oracle connection scopes (RMS and Senior ERP), a data-driven RBAC system, and automated deployments via GitHub Actions with a self-hosted Windows runner.

roles.title: Full Stack Developer
roles.description: Developed PHP web applications, WordPress plugins/themes, and Drupal solutions for international clients including American Airlines, GSK, Edenred, Sesc, EDF, and Alvarez & Marsal. Migrated legacy PHP portals to Laravel. Worked in agile teams with JavaScript and jQuery.

roles.title: Field Technician
roles.description: Managed Data Center infrastructure for one of Brazil's largest banks.

roles.title: Mainframe Systems Analyst & Developer
roles.description: Developed and tested mission-critical systems for AT&T's billing system and Bank Safra's main system.

roles.title: Computer Technician & Private Instructor
roles.description: Computer maintenance, operating systems management, and teaching basic computing skills.

Rede Zeferino careers page listing open store positions

live.title: Rede Zeferino — Corporate Site & Careers Platform
live.description: A public corporate site that doubles as two operational tools: a store-scoped careers board where each store's HR staff manage only their own openings, and a supplier/customer contact channel that routes vendor inquiries straight to the right purchasing buyer. Built on a fully custom WordPress theme with no page-builder or form-plugin dependency.
bullets: Three-tier custom RBAC across 22 HR accounts — store-scoped "RH", store-agnostic "RH Admin", and extended Administrator caps, enforced via map_meta_cap, pre_get_posts query scoping, and admin-menu pruning
Category-based vendor routing: submissions matched against a configurable category→buyer→email map, guarded by reCAPTCHA v2, a honeypot, and a submission-timing check
Dynamic jobs board on a purpose-built custom post type, matching applications by city + role to the right store's contact email at submit time
Four custom wp-admin dashboards built without any admin-page framework, with AJAX store editing and IBGE municipality data synced via a build-time fetch script
Automated per-posting SEO metadata generation into Yoast fields, with a one-time backfill migration for existing content
Site-wide maintenance kill switch — assigning one template to the front page instantly redirects all logged-out traffic, fully reversible, no plugin required
live.note: Platform engineering by me · UX/UI by Leonardo Gardinali

BPAgenda Kanban board with task columns and permissions
BPAgenda Super Pomodoro focus timer with presets and routines

BPAgenda shared expense list with balances and settlements

live.title: BPAgenda — Shared Expense & Productivity Platform
live.description: A self-hosted suite that consolidates Splitwise-style shared-expense tracking, permission-gated collaborative kanban, a reminder-driven calendar, and focus-session tools into one installable app — in active daily use with full EN/PT-BR localization.
bullets: Automated balance engine nets four aggregated sums (bidirectional debts + settlements) into a live per-friend balance on every read — nothing cached or denormalized, so totals stay correct after edits or deletions

Three-mode splitting (equal / full / custom) with multi-month installment generation inside atomic DB transactions and a validator that cross-checks friendship, payer/participant conflicts, and split-sum overflow before persisting
Hand-rolled 4-tier permission ladder (owner/full/read-write/read) gating every kanban mutation at the controller layer, on Laravel Sanctum SPA token auth
Collaborative lists with an append-only activity log that reconstructs every add/remove/check by diffing item arrays on each save
Self-hosted CI/CD — GitHub Actions deploys to a Docker/Nginx/MySQL VPS on every push to main; frontend ships as an installable PWA with a Workbox service worker for offline-aware API caching
Self-learning categorization: a 21-category keyword-matching engine backed by a per-user "learned icon" table that remembers corrections and tracks usage

images.alt: BHelper dashboard listing the user's bands
BHelper fretboard scale visualizer with harmonic field breakdown
BHelper circle of fifths tool with chord progression suggestions

live.title: BHelper — Band Management Platform
live.description: A multi-tenant Laravel API + React SPA giving each band its own isolated workspace: Kanban task boards, collaborative setlists, a song catalog auto-enriched via external music APIs, and a public band website — all under one login with invite-code onboarding.
bullets: Header-verified multi-tenancy — every endpoint resolves the active band from a request header, checks it against verified membership server-side, and scopes every query; cross-band access returns 403 rather than trusting a client-supplied ID
OAuth (PKCE) with cross-device handoff — connect a streaming account from your phone via QR code while the desktop session polls for completion, then export a setlist directly into a new playlist
Automated metadata enrichment — new songs hydrated with BPM, key, and lyrics by orchestrating two external music APIs, with response caching to avoid redundant calls
Real-time in-browser tuner using Web Audio sampling, autocorrelation, and parabolic interpolation for sub-cent accuracy, plus a fretboard/scale visualizer covering 30+ scale types
CI/CD with deploy verification — GitHub Actions deploys on every push to main, verifying the deployed commit SHA and running a live HTTP health check before marking success
Unit-tested music-theory core — pitch detection and scale/transposition isolated into pure modules with ~390 lines of Vitest coverage, decoupled from the Web Audio/DOM layer

images.alt: Rede Zeferino internal back-office system quick-access dashboard

internal.title: Rede Zeferino — Back-Office Operations Platform
internal.description: Internal web platform for a Brazilian supermarket chain (8+ stores), centralizing retail operations: production reports, label generation, data migrations, store monitoring, and Oracle RMS/Senior ERP integrations. Built solo, end to end, on my own hand-rolled PHP MVC framework.
bullets: Multi-database abstraction layer: one app talking to local MySQL, centralized Arius MySQL, Oracle RMS (read/write), and Oracle Senior ERP
Data-driven RBAC: permission classes with per-user overrides, a centralized access gate in the routing layer, and a dynamic sidebar rendered from a functionality catalog

Weight management workflow (Balança CP): real-time weight recording with immediate Oracle MERGE persistence, sequential numbering, verification, and romaneio report generation — handling 100+ daily receiving operations
Real-time store monitoring: network status of scales across 8+ stores via SSH with green/yellow/red indicators
CI/CD: GitHub Actions with a self-hosted Windows runner and PowerShell-automated zero-downtime deployments
Docker dev environment with Oracle Instant Client, MariaDB, Apache, and one-command startup
Dark/light theme system (CSS custom properties + Bootstrap 5.3 data-bs-theme, localStorage persistence, OS preference detection)

internal.title: Custom PHP MVC Framework
internal.description: Object-oriented PHP MVC framework built from scratch to master OOP best practices: centralized routing, autoloading, multi-database abstraction, Ajax support, and a Dockerized environment with automated port detection. Powers the Zeferino platform in production.

internal.title: American Airlines Internal Portal
internal.description: Built a complete internal portal from a clean WordPress installation connected to SQL Server. Developed all plugins and the custom theme, including a flexible page builder, admin dashboard for user group control, slides, polls, and menus.

internal.title: SourceLink Legacy Migration
internal.description: Migrated a legacy procedural PHP portal to Laravel MVC, restructuring the codebase with object-oriented principles.
